candidate_summary: Bauschinger effect (cyclic plasticity / kinematic hardening with back stress) → predictive model of RLHF asymmetric softening under cyclic safety-helpfulness pushes
reasoning: Mechanical rule: 19 hits, 10 forced. The 10 forced hits are materials-science source-domain pages (Wikipedia, modern-physics.org, neural-network-models-of-Bauschinger papers). 9 agent-judged hits are substantive LLM/RLHF prior art on the same phenomenon: arxiv 2504.09757 (asymmetric softening of aligned vs harmful direction), 2502.17424 (Emergent Misalignment from narrow fine-tuning), 2602.21420 (Asymmetric Confidence Penalties — explicit name overlap), 2502.11555 (Equilibrate RLHF helpfulness-safety oscillation), 2508.07172 (SafeGrad gradient-surgery for conflicting gradients), 2310.03693 (foundational fine-tuning-compromises-safety), 2601.08842 (Resisting Correction in RLHF), 2601.07200 (Push-Pull Distributional Alignment), and Nature 2024 Loss of Plasticity in Deep Continual Learning. Verdict FAIL: the asymmetric-softening / cumulative-cyclic-push phenomenon is heavily studied in 2024-2026 RLHF/alignment literature under names other than Bauschinger; the Bauschinger framing would be a label and partial mechanistic formalization rather than a new finding.
